item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.5 No.17-22, 1957
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No. 17-22 (101-106) Vol. V 1957
. . . nothing affirmeth and therefore never lyeth . . . Sir Philip Sidney (1580)
ROYAL VISIT TO AMERICA
This Sixfold Issue Costs £1. 1s. ($3.00)
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s.6d. (50 CENTS) A COPY. YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES WITH FOLDER AND INDEXED TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £4. 4s ($12 or equivalent). SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA.
1 sh. Paper only. CONTINENTAL SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME.
Lithographed in Great Britain by CAPS Ltd Dean Street, London W1, and published by Topolski's Chronicle Ltd, James Beddow Anst. 168 Tottenham W.C.4 London S.E.1. COPYRIGHT BERGQUIST
sketch_descriptions: Several loosely sketched groups of people facing each other, with some characters in profile and one figure in the center wearing a shirt that reads 'North Carolina' and waving items in both hands.
A side profile sketch of a woman with styled hair facing right, positioned prominently near the center.
A larger side profile sketch of a stern man with distinctive facial features, along with several smaller faces and figures clustered near the right edge.
pages.page_summary: The page is a 1957 issue of Topolski's Chronicle featuring a depiction of a Royal Visit to America, including expressive sketches of crowds and individuals with references to North Carolina and a quote from Sir Philip Sidney.

pages.handwritten_text:
Monday, October 21, 1957 - 3.30 P.M. – 2.55 P.M.  Ceremony of Parliament Opening by Governor General in House of Commons in main place
"Under the Peace Tower"
GOLD SAVE
Twenty-Third Parliament, Ottawa
Parliament, Ottawa
sketch_descriptions: A sketch group of figures wearing long dresses and hats, possibly parade-goers or officials, near the Parliament building.
A detailed scene showing the Parliament building with various figures attending a ceremonial event with flags and ornate decorations.
A seated figure with a wide hat viewed from the back, with several other figures nearby, some standing and some seated, interpreted as people attending the event 'Under the Peace Tower'.
A lone figure in a top hat standing by a decorative structure or carriage on the left side of the page.
pages.page_summary: This page contains expressive sketches documenting the ceremonial opening of the Canadian Parliament on October 21, 1957, with handwritten annotations describing the event and location.

pages.handwritten_text:
À l'occasion de la visite de Sa Majesté La Reine Elizabeth II et son Altesse Royale le Prince Philippe une réception civique à l'Hôtel de Ville de la Cité de Hull octobre 15 - 10 h 10-10:20 a.m.
2:15 p.m. octobre 15 - In Grounds of Government House, Ottawa
Felix Topolski
Inauguration of the Queen's Own Rifles, Ottawa, October 1957
sketch_descriptions: A group of people at a civic reception including a seated woman and standing men and women with flags bearing fleur-de-lys motifs above them.
A gathering of people outdoors around a tree in the grounds of Government House, Ottawa, with a soldier standing prominently and a photographer taking pictures on the left.
A parade or procession scene featuring military personnel, mounted soldiers, and four standing officers in uniform, related to the inauguration of the Queen's Own Rifles in Ottawa.

events: Reception for Queen Elizabeth II and Prince Philippe
Inauguration of the Queen's Own Rifles

pages.page_summary: This page contains sketches and notes documenting Queen Elizabeth II and Prince Philippe's civic reception at Hull City Hall and the inauguration of the Queen's Own Rifles in Ottawa in October 1957.

pages.handwritten_text:
M Opening of Parliament by Her Majesty The Queen. 14th October 1957
Government Reception at Charterhouse Lancisr. outlooks. over 1200 forms 5:30 - 7:30 P.M.
Reporters for the "Women's Page" watch from above.
sketch_descriptions: Upper left sketch: group of distinguished figures including men in formal military or aristocratic attire and women in elegant dresses, likely attending a formal event.
Upper right sketch: close-up of a formal interaction where an elderly woman in a cloak is being greeted or honored by a man in a top hat and formal suit.
Lower left sketch: a large, crowded reception scene with many loosely outlined figures mingling inside a hall or outdoor setting.
Lower right sketch: group of women seated and observing the scene, identified as reporters for the 'Women's Page' watching from above, with a crowd visible in the background.
pages.page_summary: This page captures sketches and notes depicting the Opening of Parliament by Queen Elizabeth II on 14th October 1957, including a government reception at Charterhouse and reporters covering the event.

pages.typeset_text:
U.S.A.

The Military Air Transport Service Terminal
WASHINGTON NATIONAL AIRPORT 11:15 A.M. OCTOBER 17

October 18th outside United States Capitol

Watching the procession on TV in a Drug Store

October 20th noon - Panthers doing march hand to hold - march with signs @ 14th Street
pages.handwritten_text:
dig that cool cat! he nearly

Watching the procession on TV in a drug-store
sketch_descriptions: A busy scene at the Military Air Transport Service Terminal at Washington National Airport, showing military personnel and other figures standing with flags and a man seated, dated October 17.
A group of people outside the United States Capitol on October 18, some holding umbrellas and appearing to participate in or observe a crowd event.
A loose figure drawing of people watching a parade or procession on TV in a drug store, with a focus on two figures mid-conversation.

Four smaller sketches showing various views of a procession or march, one with the caption 'WELCOME DEAR ELDERS', and the final one showing a Panthers march with people holding signs, dated October 20 at noon.

events: The Military Air Transport Service Terminal event

pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle depicts various scenes from the United States in October, including military personnel at an airport, crowds at the United States Capitol, people watching a procession on TV, and a Panthers march with signs.

pages.handwritten_text:
oct 16th 3:55-4:55 p.m. James Fort - The three Ships Pier
Waiting for the Visitors      Old car rally process     Williamsburg, Virginia
Oct 16th 3:55-4 p.m. The Queen and the Philip who is convoys a Kelsey the very high Colby & William and Mary
sketch_descriptions: Several figures walking and standing near three ships docked at a pier with the sun shining above and birds flying.

A small procession of people, some holding flags, along with early 20th century cars and a lamppost with signs pointing to Richmond and Jamstown, labeled as Old Car Rally Process in Williamsburg, Virginia.

A group of seated and standing people around a table with microphones, apparently during an event or meeting, captioned referring to the Queen and the Philip with a convoy and named individuals, possibly Mary and William.
pages.page_summary: This page features sketches depicting a historical event at James Fort with three ships, an old car rally procession in Williamsburg, Virginia, and a gathering involving the Queen and other notable figures.

pages.handwritten_text:
The Court of Welcome, Jamestown Festival Park  Ockwells 3:15 p.m.
WASHINGTON  at the British Embassy Reception
sketch_descriptions: A large crowd scene at an outdoor place with many people gathered in the foreground and background, some holding flags or banners, and officers present; a policeman stands prominently at right. The scene likely represents a welcoming event or parade.
A group of formally dressed people in an indoor setting, likely at a social or diplomatic reception, with some individuals seated and others standing and interacting in conversation.
pages.page_summary: The page depicts two distinct scenes: a welcoming event or festival at Jamestown Festival Park with a large crowd and flags, and a formal reception at the British Embassy in Washington with a group of guests socializing.

pages.handwritten_text:
Octbr 18th 5:00 P.M. The Heads of Mission & the Commonwealth Countries Give a Reception in Honor of Her Majesty at The British Embassy, WASHINGTON
Oct 19. 4:30 P.M. The Queen and Prince Philip receive the Heads of Diplomatic Missions in Washington
sketch_descriptions: Sketch of a group of people at a formal reception, with some individuals standing and one seated; scene labeled as a reception at the British Embassy in Washington.
Close-up sketch of a group of formally dressed men and women, some wearing hats, engaged in conversation.
Sketch of people in formal attire, seen from the side or back, some standing closely together and engaging in conversation.
Larger group sketch showing multiple men and women in formal dress mingling at an event, identified as the Queen and Prince Philip receiving Heads of Diplomatic Missions.

events: Reception in Honor of Her Majesty
Reception by the Queen and Prince Philip for Heads of Diplomatic Missions

pages.page_summary: This page depicts a series of sketches capturing formal receptions held in Washington, D.C., featuring the Queen, Prince Philip, and diplomatic heads at the British Embassy around October 18-19.

pages.handwritten_text:
The Royal Ceremony of Prem Ratty at the Nottingham Rochester Station 5:00 p.m. in honour of Her Majesty Queen and Prince Philip and the Stanley Hotel. October 17
Wes Farmer last permanent painter. - "VOC II"
sketch_descriptions: Top-left sketch depicts a crowded scene with many standing figures viewed mostly from behind, possibly at a ceremonial or public event, with a rope barrier and some figures wearing hats.
Bottom-left sketch shows a group of individuals, mostly women in hats, with two men in front; the scene appears to be indoors and participants are facing mostly right, one man facing away.

Right-side large sketch features a close-up view of two standing figures, one slightly hunched wearing a large hat and heavy clothing, the other stands upright wearing a skirt and heels, seen from the back.
pages.page_summary: The page documents scenes and figures related to the "Royal Ceremony of Prem Ratty" held on October 17 at Nottingham Rochester Station, honoring Queen and Prince Philip, including observational sketches of attendees and locations.

pages.handwritten_text:
Monday, Oct 21, 10:35 a.m. Departure from Staten Island aboard the U.S. Army ferry for the BATTERY, MANHATTAN, NEW YORK CITY
11:10 a.m. THE MOTORCADE proceeds to CITY HALL
ving doll
sketch_descriptions: Left page sketch: A view from Staten Island showing people aboard a ferry with the name 'P.W. I. Nichols' visible, various boats and ships in the water, airships and birds flying in the sky, and the Manhattan skyline in the background.
Right page sketch: A narrow street scene in Manhattan with tall buildings, a crowd of people, and flags flying above.
pages.page_summary: This page depicts a scene from a ferry departing Staten Island to Manhattan, New York City, with sketches illustrating the ferry trip, the Manhattan skyline, and a motorcade proceeding to City Hall.

pages.handwritten_text:
October 21, 3:15 p.m. The Queen and Prince Philip at the United Nations
October 21, 4:15 p.m. Outside the United Nations Building
FREE KENYA FROM EUROPEAN RULE
AFRICA FOR AFRICANS
UNITED NATIONS HELP US END SOVIET OPPRESSION AND BRITISH IRON REGIME DISSOLUTION TELLING THE PEOPLE OF ALBANIA POLAND BULGARIA ROMANIA ESTONIA TCHURSOSKA HUNGARIAN UKRAINE LATVIA LITHUANIA
October 21, 8:00 p.m. The Ridings meeting Canadian, speaking Union give dinner in honor of H.M. the K.H. at the Waldorf Astoria
sketch_descriptions: Sketch of the Queen and Prince Philip seated in a large semi-circular assembly hall (the United Nations) with many attendees.

Street scene outside the United Nations Building depicting a protest or demonstration with signs reading 'FREE KENYA FROM EUROPEAN RULE' and 'AFRICA FOR AFRICANS' and a United Nations help plea listing countries under Soviet oppression.
Crowded indoor meeting scene identified as the Ridings meeting with Canadian attendee, showing many people engaged closely.
Sketch of a solitary figure sitting on a chair seen from the back, wearing a wide hat.
Sketch of a vehicle labeled 'DOMINION WIDE PHOTOGRAPHS' parked near a press stand with 'PRESS THROUGH USE RED CAR MATCH' sign.
pages.page_summary: This page features sketches and notes capturing events around the United Nations on October 21, including the presence of the Queen and Prince Philip, a protest outside the UN building, and a Ridings meeting dinner at the Waldorf Astoria.
